extracted_value: Entity incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company, registered partnership firm, limited liability partnership, multi-state cooperative society, or cooperative society; within 10 years of incorporation or registration, or 20 years for a recognised Deep Tech Startup; turnover in any financial year since incorporation or registration not exceeding INR 200 crore, or INR 300 crore for a Deep Tech Startup; working towards innovation, development or improvement of products, processes or services, or operating a scalable business model with high employment-generation or wealth-creation potential; and not formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business.
evidence_text: G.S.R. 108(E) defines eligible legal forms, age and turnover limits, innovation criteria, Deep Tech limits and the reconstruction exclusion.

extracted_value: Enable eligible DPIIT-recognised startups to obtain certification for claiming the deduction available under Section 80-IAC of the Income-tax Act, 1961.
evidence_text: The official form describes Section 80-IAC as an incentive allowing eligible startups to obtain tax-exemption eligibility.

extracted_value: Applicant must be a DPIIT-recognised startup constituted as a private limited company or limited liability partnership, incorporated on or after 1 April 2016, with turnover not exceeding INR 100 crore in any financial year since incorporation, and must satisfy the conditions under Section 80-IAC. The business must not have been formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business or by transfer of previously used plant or machinery, except where statutory exceptions apply.
evidence_text: The official form requires DPIIT recognition, Private Limited Company or LLP status, incorporation on or after 1 April 2016, age below ten years, turnover below INR 100 crore, innovation or scalability, and no reconstruction of an existing business.

extracted_value: DPIIT-recognised private limited companies and limited liability partnerships that satisfy Section 80-IAC eligibility conditions.
evidence_text: The official eligibility section limits applicants to DPIIT-recognised Private Limited Companies and Limited Liability Partnerships meeting the stated conditions.

extracted_value: Eligible startups receiving certification may claim a deduction of 100 percent of profits and gains derived from the eligible business for three consecutive assessment years selected from the first ten years since incorporation, subject to the Income-tax Act.
evidence_text: The official page states that eligible startups can avail a 100 percent tax deduction for three consecutive financial years within their first ten years.
review_notes: Actual tax claims remain subject to the Income-tax Act and applicable assessment requirements.

extracted_value: Obtain DPIIT Startup Recognition first; open the official Form 80-IAC application on the Startup India portal; complete the application and declarations; upload the applicable supporting documents; submit the application to the Inter-Ministerial Board through the DPIIT portal; and monitor the application status online.
evidence_text: The official form requires confirmation of recognition details, completion of the 80-IAC form and evaluation sections, uploading documents, accepting the terms and submitting the application.

extracted_value: Applicable documents include incorporation or registration evidence, shareholding details, board resolution where applicable, income-tax return acknowledgements, audited financial statements for the applicable years, chartered-accountant certifications and declarations concerning formation and financial information, and supporting material such as the pitch deck and application video as required by the official form.
evidence_text: The official form lists shareholding details, resolutions, income-tax returns, audited statements, CA certifications, pitch deck and other supporting evidence, with some items conditional on the applicant's circumstances.
review_notes: Mandatory and conditional items require clearer public presentation.
